violations.text: Soap and paper towels were not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026

Beef Sparerib, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Food item was observed held at 48 degrees Fahrenheit and was voluntarily disposed of during the time of inspection. Cooling methods reviewed during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of various dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Bowls were removed during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026
Utensils (Rice scoop) were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Utensils in between use shall be kept on a dry clean surface. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026
Utensils (knives) were observed stored in between the prep unit and other equipment between uses. Utensils shall be kept on a dry clean surface. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the large chest freezer and wok cookline have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to prevent the accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026
A few ceiling tiles in the back prep area observed covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. Ceiling tiles shall be replaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Paper towels must be provided at all times. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of various dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Bowls were removed during time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025
Utensils (Rice scoop) were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Utensils in between uses shall be kept on a dry clean surface. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025
Utensils (knives) were observed stored in between the prep unit and other equipment between uses. Utensils shall be kept on a dry clean surface. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the hood ventilation system, walk-in cooler fan guards, and floors in kitchen have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to prevent the accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025
A few ceiling tiles in the back prep area observed covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. Ceiling tiles shall be replaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024

Raw animal foods (such as raw beef and raw chicken) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as cooked pork and cooked egg rolls) in the walk-in refrigerator and in the front small refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. -Staff moved the raw chicken and raw beef away from ready to eat foods during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/29/2023
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. -Staff removed the stagnant water during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/29/2023
The bottom door panel of the walk-in cooler and top panel of the hood top station observed not in good repair. Equipment shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the areas underneath the fryolator have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. The non-food contact surfaces shall be cleaned as often as necessary to prevent buildup of residue. New Violation. Correct By: 01/12/2024
A few ceiling tiles in the back prep area observed covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. Ceiling tiles shall be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024
